hypothesis: Incorporating temporal degradation dynamics into foundation model representations—by pre-training on sequential SMILES trajectories that encode electrochemical reaction intermediates and decomposition products rather than static molecular structures—will enable prediction of battery cycle life and capacity fade as emergent multi-scale properties, with models trained on such dynamic representations outperforming static SMILES-based foundation models specifically in out-of-distribution generalization to novel electrolyte chemistries where long-term stability diverges substantially from initial performance metrics.
